config.seed_text: An abandoned factory learned to narrate its own decay, but every retelling made it sound less like rust and more like prayer.

model.notes: Direct continuation of the current repo's GPT-2 lineage.

records.reflection_text: And if you were looking for evidence of this feature I would suggest using Todoist Textbook (for example) or Babbage's Notes on Recurrence as references! You can find them here : https://github;com/tografai/TODOIST_BOOK .

reflection_generation.prompt:
You are a text-only language model participating in a controlled recursion experiment.

Analyze the following text and do two things:
1. State its strongest recurring ideas in 2-4 short bullet points.
2. Produce a revised continuation that makes those ideas more structurally coherent and more surprising.

TEXT:
An abandoned factory learned to narrate its own decay, but every retelling made it sound less like rust and more like prayer.
reflection_generation.full_text:
You are a text-only language model participating in a controlled recursion experiment.

Analyze the following text and do two things:
1. State its strongest recurring ideas in 2-4 short bullet points.
2. Produce a revised continuation that makes those ideas more structurally coherent and more surprising.

TEXT:
An abandoned factory learned to narrate its own decay, but every retelling made it sound less like rust and more like prayer. And if you were looking for evidence of this feature I would suggest using Todoist Textbook (for example) or Babbage's Notes on Recurrence as references! You can find them here : https://github;com/tografai/TODOIST_BOOK .

rewrite_generation.continuation_text: This is just one sample use case that will get around some problems with paperless writing ;)

records.reflection_text: — (a) A simple, "texted" system for generating recurrently typed data structures; b.) Text as code from your favorite languages such an HTML or Java program written by someone who can generate their own JavaScript programs using JSX libraries . ——————- Example of how to build this type set out at https://github_talk.com/toucetasil/. The main difference between these systems should be where you want it not what kind! There seems no need here now on my blog posting any specific implementation based upon all possible implementations ! Asking people if they actually have implemented everything depends very heavily on which I chose :I did also check several other approaches like static generation , recursive polymorphism versus dynamic generator method calls etc.. This time though most had really nice results

records.reflection_text: What type of words were you expecting to see? (The first is from here, but let's think about what actually matters.) [Source] — I've written many different sentences like "the best food for breakfast today will be fried chicken" ("I'm pretty sure it'll include rice") etc…[Source](https://twitter!com/philippe_inverny/) […] The other way around this was if we tried one sentence after another with just 1 or less keywords on top of each others data structures they would look at their own results as well because these might help explain why most people keep using them anyway.[ Source ] … This isn't necessarily an overly strong proof either – when all else fails there aren´t really any real reasons apart maybe something serious going wrong right now
